nodes.title: Establish repository, git, and GitHub prerequisites for qd execution
nodes.spec:
## Objective
Establish the repository-level prerequisites the SDD-derived DAG assumes for a greenfield workspace: instantiate this project as a valid local git worktree rooted at the project, verify GitHub CLI availability/auth, and either verify the target GitHub repository workflow or record a typed blocker if the remote repository has not yet been created.

## Non-goals
Implementing picoMCP features.

## SDD design slice
Support/process precondition for all components; qd config requires a real worktree and an honest CI/PR surface before downstream evidence can be trusted.

## Behaviors this node must prove
- bootstrap/repository-is-a-valid-git-worktree
- bootstrap/github-cli-is-available-for-ci-and-pr-operations

## Tasks
- [ ] Instantiate or repair the project root into a usable local git worktree for qd and document branch/remotes.
- [ ] Verify GitHub CLI is installed and authenticated.
- [ ] If the target GitHub repository already exists, verify CI/PR commands against that repository; otherwise record a typed external-dependency blocker for remote repository creation rather than faking success.
- [ ] Record the canonical repository root and any host/shell path caveats that affect qd commands in this greenfield environment.
- [ ] Terminal: run the bootstrap verification commands against the real local repo and capture their output as evidence; capture either successful remote proof or blocker evidence for the missing remote.

## Real-world dependencies
Host shell access to git and gh; if the workspace starts greenfield, git metadata must be created here before downstream qd work begins. Remote GitHub repository creation is an external dependency if CI/PR workflow evidence is required.

## Rollback
Restore prior git metadata/remotes if repository owners reject the instantiated bootstrap state.
nodes.acceptance: The greenfield project workspace is instantiated or repaired into a valid local git worktree from the execution environment, GitHub CLI authentication is verified, and either the configured GitHub repository workflow is proven with real commands or a typed external-dependency blocker is recorded for missing remote repository creation.

nodes.title: Configure qd gates, merge policy, and export settings for this repository
nodes.spec:
## Objective
Configure qd with honest repository gates before any implementation node is considered ready: check command, CI command, merge strategy, and deterministic export path.

## Non-goals
Executing CI or merging code.

## SDD design slice
Process support around all components; the DAG is dishonest if qd points at empty or fake gates.

## Behaviors this node must prove
- bootstrap/qd-check-and-ci-gates-are-configured
- bootstrap/qd-deterministic-export-is-configured

## Tasks
- [ ] Determine the repository's real fast preflight command and full trusted CI gate.
- [ ] Set qd check-command, ci-command, merge-strategy, and export output deterministically.
- [ ] Verify qd doctor and config reflect the real gates rather than placeholders.
- [ ] Terminal: run the real qd config and doctor commands and capture the resulting configuration as evidence.

## Real-world dependencies
Depends on bootstrap-repo-git-gh because the repo and CI surface must be known first.

## Rollback
Restore prior .qd/config.toml if repository owners reject the configured gates.

nodes.title: Verify runtime environment prerequisites for PICO-8-backed work
nodes.spec:
## Objective
Verify the real runtime prerequisites named by the SDD for runtime/export work: usable PICO-8 binary discovery, version/capability probe, and Linux headless support such as Xvfb where needed.

## Non-goals
Implementing runtime_svc itself.

## SDD design slice
config/capability + runtime_svc external surface research from SDD §6.1, §7.7, §7.8, §7.9, §13.

## Behaviors this node must prove
- bootstrap/pico8-runtime-surface-is-verified-or-blocked
- bootstrap/headless-linux-support-is-verified-or-blocked

## Tasks
- [ ] Verify how PICO-8 is discovered in this environment and whether a licensed binary is actually available.
- [ ] Verify the headless execution path required by the host OS (for example xvfb-run on Linux).
- [ ] If unavailable, create typed qd blockers for provider/environment prerequisites rather than pretending runtime work is provable.
- [ ] Terminal: run the real capability discovery/probe commands and capture the resulting evidence or blocker references.

## Real-world dependencies
Depends on bootstrap-repo-git-gh because host repo context and shell access must be established first.

## Rollback
N/A; this is research/blocking state.

nodes.title: Boot-to-menu game collection: Pong, Snake, and Flappy Bird clone
nodes.spec:
## Objective
Prove picoMCP end-to-end by authoring a boot-to-menu game collection cartridge entirely through the picoMCP CLI/MCP surface. The cartridge boots to a selector menu and launches one of three small games: Pong (vs computer), Snake, or a Flappy Bird clone.

## Non-goals
Polished game mechanics, sound effects, or artistic sprites beyond functional minimums.

## SDD design slice
All picoMCP surfaces: cart_repo, static_svc, asset_svc, runtime_svc, cli_adapter, mcp_adapter.

## Behaviors this node must prove
- menu-driven-game-collection/cartridge-boots-to-a-selector-menu
- menu-driven-game-collection/menu-lets-the-player-choose-pong-snake-or-flappy
- menu-driven-game-collection/pong-runs-vs-computer-with-scoring
- menu-driven-game-collection/snake-runs-with-self-collision-ending-the-game
- menu-driven-game-collection/flappy-bird-clone-runs-with-obstacle-avoidance
- menu-driven-game-collection/each-game-returns-to-the-menu-on-game-over
- menu-driven-game-collection/all-games-fit-together-in-one-cartridge-under-token-and-compressed-size-limits

## Tasks
- [ ] Use picoMCP CLI to create the cartridge and write Lua code for the menu screen
- [ ] Use picoMCP CLI to write Lua code for Pong (player paddle, computer paddle, ball, scoring)
- [ ] Use picoMCP CLI to write Lua code for Snake (growing body, self-collision detection)
- [ ] Use picoMCP CLI to write Lua code for Flappy Bird clone (gravity, obstacle pipes, scoring)
- [ ] Use picoMCP CLI to draw simple sprites for each game (paddles, ball, snake segments, bird, pipes)
- [ ] Use picoMCP CLI to wire the menu selection to each game's init function
- [ ] Use picoMCP CLI to parse and lint the final cartridge
- [ ] Use picoMCP CLI to report cartridge size and verify it fits within PICO-8 limits
- [ ] Use picoMCP CLI to run each game headlessly and capture screenshots as evidence
- [ ] Terminal: prove every behavior end-to-end via picoMCP CLI commands with real cartridge output

## Real-world dependencies
Depends on all 22 picoMCP DAG nodes (reading, writing, editing, parsing, linting, minifying, size-reporting, sprites, map, sound effects, running headlessly, exporting). Requires a working PICO-8 binary and xvfb-run.

## Rollback
Delete the output cartridge and its screenshots.
nodes.acceptance: the cartridge boots to a menu with three game choices selecting Pong starts an interactive paddle-vs-computer match with scoring and game-over return to menu selecting Snake starts the snake game with food collection and self-collision ending the game selecting Flappy Bird starts the bird-flying game with scrolling pipes and collision detection each game returns to the menu on game over the entire cartridge fits within token and compressed size limits
